refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR focuses on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It is already solution-neutral and does not specify how to achieve this, only what is required. There are no multiple obligations to split.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.

candidate_dps: Implement adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within limits
Use error detection and correction mechanisms to recover lost packets and ensure communication quality
Design redundant communication paths to provide failover and reduce effective packet loss
Incorporate real-time network quality monitoring and feedback control to dynamically optimize data flow
Apply data compression and prioritization to reduce bandwidth usage and minimize packet loss impact
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Dependency on real-time network monitoring data
Interaction between adaptive protocol and error correction mechanisms if combined
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a logical solution that can operate independently by adjusting transmission parameters based on network conditions. It avoids coupling with specific hardware or redundant paths, supporting modularity.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate real-time network condition data
Risk that adaptive protocol complexity may increase system design and testing effort
Potential dependency on network conditions that may vary beyond control
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including real-time network quality monitoring as a supporting DP or sub-DP to clarify data source for adaptation and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for network communication management and protocol adaptation

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used to send and receive communication status and performance data

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to the physical or logical network for data transmission

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as adaptive communication protocol is core to network handling; minimal diffusion since protocol adjustments are localized here.
Low containment as vehicle module focuses on physical and control systems; would require extensive diffusion to handle adaptive protocol.
Partial containment possible since teleoperation depends on communication, but adaptive protocol logic is more network-centric.
Poor containment; safety services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, not communication protocol adaptation.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the adaptive communication protocol DP, as it directly manages network communication and can encapsulate protocol adaptation with minimal diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on telemetry data from other modules may cause minor coupling.
Changes in network hardware interfaces might require coordination with Vehicle or Teleoperation modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by implementing an adaptive communication protocol that monitors and adjusts transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within the customer-defined limit, fulfilling the requirement for teleoperation communication quality.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on telemetry data from other modules may cause minor coupling.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and minimal diffusion risk, as it centralizes the adaptive communication protocol logic. Other modules have low containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the appropriate IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry interface and ensuring compliance with the ≤1% packet loss acceptance criterion under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and appropriate, with well-defined interfaces to Telemetry and Network Link supporting the implementation and verification of the FR.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and manage dependencies on telemetry data from other modules to minimize coupling and maintain modularity.
Establish coordination protocols with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to handle changes in network hardware interfaces, reducing integration risks.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is adequately addressed by the design parameter and properly allocated to the implementation module. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed, and the adaptive communication protocol effectively meets the packet loss requirement. Although minor coupling risks exist due to telemetry dependencies, these are manageable with recommended revisions and do not necessitate further decomposition at this stage. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and verified design is paramount, and the current level satisfies these criteria.

revise.why: To monitor and manage dependencies on telemetry data from other modules to minimize coupling and maintain modularity, and to establish coordination protocols with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to handle changes in network hardware interfaces, reducing integration risks.

assumptions: The adaptive communication protocol can maintain packet loss ≤1% under agreed network conditions.
Telemetry data dependencies can be effectively managed to avoid significant coupling.
open_risks: Potential dependency on telemetry data from other modules causing minor coupling.
Integration risks due to changes in network hardware interfaces.
evidence_needed: Monitoring data showing packet loss metrics consistently within acceptance criteria.
Verification reports confirming the effectiveness of coordination protocols with related modules.
